source: Ash feature request 2026-06-21 â€” guests get full tools EXCEPT file edits, and execution needs Ash approval; gates must be unbypassable.
title: Profiles: code-enforced guest capability gates (no file edits, exec needs approval, no god)
current_state: agent/tools.py::execute_tool dispatches any registered tool with no per-caller capability check. Tool access is gated only by ModeConfig.tool_allowlist (used by god mode). Nothing distinguishes a guest caller; god mode is enterable by anyone who types the trigger.
target_state: Capability enforcement lives in the dispatch layer, keyed off agent.current_profile.is_guest â€” NOT in the prompt (prompts are advisory and bypassable). For a guest: (a) file-write tools (modify_file, create_file, and any fs-mutating tool) are DENIED with a clear message; (b) execution tools (execute_python, execute_bash, and the like) do NOT run â€” they return a 'queued for Ash approval' result handed to the T-225 workflow; (c) god mode entry is refused outright. Everything else (chat, web/scholar search, weather/news/stocks, image gen, the full 3-tier memory + recall) works normally in the guest's own namespace. A neutral, warm guest consciousness prompt (prompts/consciousness_guest.txt) replaces the Ash-specific one.
migration_plan: 1. Define capability sets in agent/profile.py: GUEST_DENIED_TOOLS (file writes), GUEST_APPROVAL_TOOLS (execution). Keep these as explicit named frozensets next to the Profile class for auditability.

2. execute_tool: before running, if agent.current_profile and current_profile.is_guest: if tool in GUEST_DENIED_TOOLS -> return structured denial; if tool in GUEST_APPROVAL_TOOLS -> hand off to request_execution_approval (T-225) and return a 'pending approval' result. Default-deny posture: a tool that mutates the filesystem and isn't explicitly classified is treated as denied (fail closed).
3. God gate: refuse god-mode entry / god namespace whenever current_profile != ash. Put this check at the mode-switch site AND assert it in profile_to_memory so a guest can never resolve the god db.
4. New prompts/consciousness_guest.txt: friendly, respectful, generic assistant persona (NO Ash-loyalty, NO competition framing). Selected by T-223 for guest profiles.

5. Tests: testing/test_guest_gates.py â€” guest modify_file/create_file denied; guest execute_python/bash returns pending-approval (does NOT execute); guest cannot enter god mode or resolve god/pi memory; guest CAN use memory_read/write, web_search, get_weather, image gen; an unclassified fs-mutating tool fails closed.
risk_notes: This is the security core: enforce in code, fail closed, and unit-test the denials directly. The guest prompt is cosmetic defense-in-depth, never the gate. Double-check there is no alternate execution path (e.g. a tool that shells out internally) that sidesteps GUEST_APPROVAL_TOOLS â€” classify by capability, not just by tool name where feasible.
